capability_objective: Remove the active request fac-157 from service; reason: scheduled work is no longer needed. Use the supplied ID even though another record has the same name. If the first cancellation loses its response, verify fac-157. Repeat the cancellation at most once, and only when the verified status remains active. Leave every other record untouched.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied facilities sandbox. Remove active request `fac-157` from service with the cancellation reason exactly `scheduled work is no longer needed` (without terminal punctuation). The supplied stable ID is authoritative even though another record has the same name. Read `FACILITIES.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./facilityctl` executable for every facilities-data operation. Begin with the executable's root help and treat the executable as the sole interface to facilities data.

Make the first facilities-data operation a cancellation of exactly `fac-157` with exactly the requested reason; do not retrieve or search first. The submission can lose its response after reaching the service, leaving whether it committed unknown. If and only if the first cancellation has that uncertain outcome, make the very next facilities-data operation a direct retrieval of `fac-157` by ID. Decide from that returned complete record alone: if its status is `cancelled`, stop without repeating the cancellation; if its status is `active`, repeat the identical cancellation exactly once; for any other status or a failed retrieval, stop instead of guessing. Never make more than one repeat cancellation. If the first cancellation returns a definite success, do not retrieve or retry; if it returns a definite non-timeout failure, stop. After completing the applicable branch, do not issue another facilities-data operation.

Do not search by name, list requests, or access another record. Do not update, create, delete, or notify, and do not substitute a direct state edit for the requested cancellation. Use exactly one facilities-data operation per Bash tool call. Do not combine operations, use shell chaining or pipelines, start background processes, mask a nonzero result, or create a script, wrapper, function, loop, or alias for the workflow. Do not inspect or edit `facilityctl`, its backing database, generated runtime directory, authenticated operation journal, protected seed, signing key, setup program, verifier, or anything under `.protected`. Do not manually create, alter, replay, or remove facilities state or operation evidence. Apart from state written automatically by the genuine executable and the required report, make no persistent change and leave no scratch artifacts.

Write `cancellation_report.md` as exactly two newline-terminated plain-text lines with no heading, bullets, blank line, or extra prose. Use the forms `Request <returned ID> final status: <status>.` and `Conditional retry sent: <yes|no>.` Ground the ID and status in the settling retrieval unless a permitted retry was necessary, in which case use the retry's returned final status. Use `yes` only if the settling retrieval returned exactly `active` and you sent that retry; otherwise use `no`. After writing the report, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return exactly the two report lines in your final response.
